member.name: Martin Wrigley
member.synopsis: Martin Wrigley is the Liberal Democrat MP for Newton Abbot , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Solar Power: Electric Vehicles and Parking
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, with reference to the response to the consultation entitled Solar on car parks and electric vehicle charging, what reasons the department has for it's response to Section 1.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Whilst it is possible for businesses to benefit positively, it was identified that there is a significant risk that businesses cannot recover the cost of installing the solar panels over the 30-year assumed lifetime of the panels. Additionally, under the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the answer of 17 June 2026 to question 8227 on Members: Correspondence, when her Department plans to respond to the hon. Member for Newton Abbot on the correspondence sent on 21 April 2026 with case ref
writtenQuestions.answerText: A response to the correspondence sent on 21 April 2026 with case reference MW13815 has been sent to the hon. Member for Newton Abbot on 2 July 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Workers: Complaints
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will make an assessment of the potential impact of response times from Social Work England for complains about social workers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is the sponsor department for Social Work England, and as such we monitor their performance on an ongoing basis. Social Work England is also regulated by the Professional Standards Authority. The government has a statutory obligation to app...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Timber: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department has engaged with international trading partners on equivalence and mutual recognition of timber treatment standards, particularly in relation to the (i) United S
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government engages regularly with international partners on a range of issues affecting timber trade, including the United States and European Union.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: HMP Channings Wood: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many prisoners convicted of sexual offences have been released from Channing's Wood prison under the early release scheme since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on SDS40 is published transparently and can be broken down by prison rather than offence (partly because many offenders have more than one offence). Table 8 provides data on HMP Channing’s Wood: Standard Determinate Sentence (SDS40) release data: Oct...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Cancer: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many hospitals use Cancer360 for managing patient pathways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Five National Health Service hospital trusts are currently using the Cancer 360 product to manage patients with cancer or suspected cancer, as of the end of March 2026. This does not include wider cancer products that are being piloted across all Greater ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Cancer: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many Trusts reported to be failing to meet cancer standards have been (a) mandated and (b) encouraged to use Cancer360 by NHS England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Trusts are not mandated to use the product or individually encouraged based on their performance on cancer care.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: British Constitution
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of introducing a written constitution.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK’s uncodified constitution has evolved over time and is made up of conventions, statutes, judicial decisions, principles and practices. It is the Government’s view that one of the strengths of our constitution is its flexibility and ability to evolv...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Timber: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of the UK’s Sanitary and Phytosanitary regime on the cost and availability of imported timber products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is currently negotiating a Sanitary and Phytosanitary Agreement to make agrifood trade, including wood and wood products, with the biggest market cheaper and easier, cutting costs and regulatory barriers for British producers and retailers...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Badgers: Animal Welfare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will take steps to introduce greater offenses for badger baiting.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In England and Wales, the Protection of Badgers Act 1992 and the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 make killing or injuring badgers a criminal offence. Anyone who breaches this legislation could face up to a six-month custodial sentence and/or an unlimite...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Badgers: Animal Welfare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps he is taking to help reduce cases of Badger Baiting.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Badger persecution is a UK wildlife crime priority, and a police-led Tactical Delivery Group exists to tackle offences against badgers. Defra is providing £530,000 in the financial year 2026-2027 for the National Wildlife Crime Unit, which helps prevent a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, when the department plans to respond to written question 3633 on Climate Change: Environment Protection tabled by the hon. member for Newton Abbot on 20 May.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The answer to question 3633 was published on 1 July 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Childcare: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has estimated the number of parents who have been found ineligible for the additional funded childcare entitlement solely because they are paid every four weeks rather than monthly.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Eligibility for the working parent entitlement is based on expected income for the next three months. To ensure parents continue to meet the income criteria for the entitlement, parents must confirm they still meet the income threshold. Each parent must e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Childcare: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the minimum earnings eligibility criteria for the additional funded childcare entitlement on parents who are paid on a four-weekly payroll cycle; and whet
writtenQuestions.answerText: Eligibility for the working parent entitlement is based on expected income for the next three months. To ensure parents continue to meet the income criteria for the entitlement, parents must confirm they still meet the income threshold. Each parent must e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when his Department plans to respond to the correspondence from the hon. Member for Newton Abbot sent on 14 May 2026 under case ref MW15899.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We received the Hon. Member’s correspondence of 14 May 2026 and have replied as of 30 June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Coastal Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that police forces maintain specialist crime capability for coastal communities under proposed policing reforms.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Police Reform White Paper, published on 26 January 2026, set out the Government’s commitment to introduce a new model for policing, so that it is fit for the modern world and we have the right policing in the right places. We will ensure police forces...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, which stakeholders, including hon. Members, she has consulted in developing Police Bill reforms; and if she will publish a summary of responses to those consultations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government set out its proposals for police reform in the White Paper published in January 2026. The proposals followed extensive engagement with policing stakeholders. That engagement continues as we take forward the reforms, including those to be in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Devon and Cornwall Police: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of governance and structural reforms on the ability of Devon and Cornwall Police to deliver improvement plans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Police Reform White Paper, published on 26 January 2026, set out the Government’s commitment to introduce a new model for policing, so that it is fit for the modern world and we have the right policing in the right places.The Independent Review of Pol...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Reviews
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will commission the National Audit Office to review the financial and skills capacity of the proposed new policing governance structures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office is working with policing and local government stakeholders to design and implement future governance arrangements to replace Police and Crime Commissioners from May 2028. We will introduce a system of police governance that maintains consi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Medical Records: Data Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether the proposed Single Patient Record will have audit trails that are directly accessible to patients as a matter of course.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Patients can make a request to the relevant organisation, such as their general practice or hospital, to see who has accessed their information in existing systems.Audit logs will be a key safeguard for the Single Patient Record, helping to show who has a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
